name: Risk Assessment — Digital Transformation
description: A mid-size company migrating from legacy systems to cloud-native. The projector maps risk correlations, reveals cascade risks, and identifies which mitigations are structural pillars vs theater.
direction: Complete migration within 12 months without business disruption while maintaining compliance and team morale

tensions.claim: Running old and new systems in parallel reduces risk but doubles infrastructure cost and splits team attention
Legacy data has 15 years of accumulated inconsistencies — cleaning during migration is necessary but delays go-live by 3 months
Cloud provider offers managed services that accelerate delivery but create dependency that limits future negotiating leverage
Current team knows legacy systems deeply but cloud-native requires skills they don't have — training vs hiring vs consulting
Cloud architecture meets current regulations but regulatory landscape is shifting and new rules may require architectural changes
Zero-downtime migration requires feature parity which takes 6 months longer than building cloud-native from scratch
Cloud expands attack surface but enables better monitoring — net security depends on team maturity not architecture
Transformation projects lose executive sponsorship after 9 months — our timeline is 12 months with no early wins planned
37 integrations with partners and internal systems — each is a potential failure point during cutover
Budget has 15% contingency but similar projects in the industry average 40% overrun — we are underestimating complexity
End users resist new workflows even when objectively better — adoption failure kills more transformations than technology failure
Rollback capability is essential but maintaining it constrains architectural decisions and adds 20% to development effort
